title: Unauthorized Firmware Upload Attempt on RTU

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 2.1' → 'CR 2.1'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=ot_sec sourcetype="claroty:alert" category="firmware" earliest=-14d
| append [ search index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" app IN ("ftp","scp","sftp") dest_zone="OT-RTU" earliest=-14d
    | lookup ot_engineering_nets.csv src OUTPUT role | where role!="ews" ]
| stats earliest(_time) as t0, values(src) as sources by dest, signature, sourcetype
| sort - t0
value: Detects firmware or OS image transfer sessions to RTUs from non-engineering hosts, testing authorization enforcement on components.
implementation: (1) Block firmware protocols except via jump host; (2) require signed images; (3) alert any ftp to field; (4) integrate vendor secure boot logs if available.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
